title: Replace naive frequency features with ordered or cross-fitted historical priors for high-cardinality categoricals
summary: The data has hundreds of drivers and many sparse interactions.
rationale: The data has hundreds of drivers and many sparse interactions. scikit-learn documents that target encoding without cross-fitting catastrophically overfits high-cardinality features, while CatBoost documents `has_time` specifically to preserve input order instead of random permutations. This directly matches the competition’s sequential driver-race structure.
implementation_hint: Sort by `Year, Race, Driver, LapNumber` and test two variants: `CatBoostClassifier` with `has_time=True` on raw categoricals, and a leakage-safe `TargetEncoder.fit_transform` pipeline for interactions like `Driver x Race`, `Driver x Compound`, `Race x Stint`, `Driver x Race x StintBin`. Prefer priors built only from past folds or past laps, not global encodings.
expected_effect: Higher signal from sparse categories without leaking future laps of the same driver/race into the present row.
risk: If folds are not strictly chronology-aware, encoded priors can look great locally and fail publicly.
